title: app_identity: TOFU check on existing peer not atomic with slot update
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_identity/app_identity.c:201-231

The TOFU key pinning check and subsequent slot update are protected by the mutex, which is good. However, the check at lines 210-215 has a subtle issue:

if (s_peers[slot].peer.has_public_key &&
    memcmp(s_peers[slot].peer.public_key, peer->public_key,
           LICHEN_APP_IDENTITY_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN) != 0) {
    k_mutex_unlock(&s_mutex);
    return -EEXIST;
}

The condition s_peers[slot].peer.has_public_key will ALWAYS be true for existing peers because:
- lichen_app_identity_upsert_peer requires has_public_key=true (lines 193-196)
- lichen_app_identity_upsert_peer_key sets has_public_key=true (line 248)

The check is defensive but misleading. If it were ever false (perhaps from uninitialized memory or a bug), TOFU would be silently bypassed - the new key would overwrite without verification.

Suggested fix: Either:
1. __builtin_unreachable() or __ASSERT() after the has_public_key check since it should be invariant
2. Return -EINTR or similar if has_public_key is unexpectedly false (defensive failure)
3. Add a comment explaining this is defensive against future changes

Not a critical bug since current code ensures has_public_key is always true, but the defensive check could be made explicitly defensive.
